bodies.name: Committee on Academic Policy and Programs

packages.title: University Affairs Board — Agenda Package · October 23, 2025
packages.summary: At its Cycle 1 meeting (Oct 23, 2025, Council Chamber, Simcoe Hall), the University Affairs Board opened with board orientation and the Report of the Senior Assessors, then worked through a heavily student-facing agenda. For information it received proposed revisions to the University's Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment plus the annual report on activity under that policy, a presentation on the Hart House Building Renewal, and the Multi-Year Accessibility Plan (MYAP). For recommendation it considered a motion to approve in principle the MacMillan Theatre Remediation capital project (~1,200 gross / 440 net assignable square metres, funded by Provostial funds), which it concurred with from the Academic Board. The consent agenda carried Report No. 247 of the May 21, 2025 meeting and the annual report on summer executive authority approvals; one item (item 15, the MacMillan Theatre capital report) was taken in camera under By-Law No. 2.
packages.gradRelevance: Multiple items hit graduate students directly. The Sexual Violence & Sexual Harassment policy revisions set the protections, supports and complaint process that cover grad students, TAs and RAs, and the annual report shows how often those supports are actually used. The Multi-Year Accessibility Plan determines accommodations available to disabled grad students, and the Hart House renewal affects a space funded partly by student fees. The MacMillan Theatre capital approval commits Provostial funds to teaching/performance space rather than other priorities.
keyItems: Board orientation and Report of the Senior Assessors (for information)
Proposed revisions to the Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment (for information)
Annual Report on activity under the SVSH Policy (for information)
Motion to approve in principle the MacMillan Theatre Remediation (~1,200 gsm / 440 nasm, Provostial funds) — for recommendation
Hart House Building Renewal project presentation (for information)
Consent agenda: Report No. 247 (May 21, 2025) and Summer Executive Authority annual report
Item 15 (MacMillan Theatre capital report) considered in camera under By-Law No. 2

documents.title: Report of the Senior Assessors (Presentation)
documents.summary: Opening administrative presentation framing the Board's Cycle 1 priorities across its student-services and campus mandate.
documents.gradRelevance: Signals the administration's agenda on the services and policies grad students rely on.

documents.title: Proposed Revisions: Policy on Sexual Violence and Sexual Harassment
documents.summary: Proposed amendments to the University-wide SVSH policy, brought for information ahead of approval — covering definitions, supports available, and the disclosure/complaint process.
documents.gradRelevance: Directly governs the protections, supports and reporting options available to graduate students, TAs and RAs.

documents.title: Annual Report: Activity under the SVSH Policy
documents.summary: Annual data on disclosures, reports and supports provided under the SVSH policy across the University over the past year.
documents.gradRelevance: Shows how the policy is actually being used and resourced for students who experience harm.

documents.title: Capital Project: MacMillan Theatre Remediation — Project Planning Report
documents.summary: Project planning report recommending approval in principle of the MacMillan Theatre remediation (~1,200 gsm / 440 nasm theatre space), to be funded by Provostial funds.
documents.gradRelevance: A capital commitment of University funds affecting teaching and performance space.

documents.title: Hart House Building Renewal (Presentation)
documents.summary: Presentation on the planned renewal of the Hart House building, its programming, and affected spaces.
documents.gradRelevance: Hart House is funded partly by student fees and used widely by graduate students.

documents.title: Multi-Year Accessibility Plan (MYAP)
documents.summary: The University's multi-year plan for meeting accessibility obligations across facilities, services and academics.
documents.gradRelevance: Determines the accommodations and accessibility supports available to disabled graduate students.

documents.title: Summer Executive Authority: Annual Report on UAB Approvals
documents.summary: Annual report on decisions approved under summer executive authority while the Board was not meeting.
documents.gradRelevance: Transparency on decisions made over the summer that can affect student services.

bodies.name: Ad Hoc Committee for Capital Projects
